title: The AI Divide: Europe’s Future and the Growing Debate Over Technology
subtitle: From banking strategies to literary controversies, the rise of artificial intelligence is changing how we work, create, and view the future.

summary.en: Europe faces growing pressure to improve its technology sector as concerns about falling behind the US and China increase. Large companies like Lloyds Banking Group are hiring hundreds of experts to integrate artificial intelligence into their daily operations. Meanwhile, the literary world is struggling with questions about whether AI is being used to create award-winning art. These developments highlight the complex challenges of managing new technology in a rapidly changing global economy. Policymakers and businesses are now debating how to balance innovation with safety and human oversight.
summary.zh: 歐洲在擔憂落後於美國與中國的情緒下，面臨提升科技產業的壓力。勞埃德銀行集團等大型企業正招募數百名專家，將人工智慧整合至日常營運中。同時，文學界正為人工智慧是否被用於創作獲獎作品而苦惱。這些發展凸顯了在瞬息萬變的全球經濟中，管理新技術所帶來的複雜挑戰。決策者與企業目前正針對如何在創新與安全及人類監督之間取得平衡進行辯論。

paragraphs.en: The debate over artificial intelligence continues to grow as new developments in technology, business, and culture emerge across the globe. Following recent government actions in the United States to restrict access to advanced AI models due to safety concerns, European leaders are facing renewed questions about their own technological progress. A viral thought experiment, known as "Europe 2031," has recently captured the attention of policymakers in Brussels. This project warns that Europe risks falling behind the US and China if it does not invest more heavily in its own AI infrastructure and digital tools. According to The Guardian, the scenario has been discussed by officials as a wake-up call, urging Europe to take faster action to protect its economic future.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著全球在科技、商業與文化領域不斷出現新進展，關於人工智慧的辯論持續升溫。繼美國政府近期因安全疑慮採取行動，限制先進人工智慧模型的取得管道後，歐洲領導人正面臨關於自身科技進步的重新檢視。一項名為「歐洲二零三一」的熱門思想實驗，近期引起布魯塞爾決策者的關注。該計畫警告，若歐洲不加強投資自身的人工智慧基礎設施與數位工具，恐將面臨落後於美國與中國的風險。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，官員們已將此情境視為一記警鐘，敦促歐洲採取更迅速的行動，以維護其經濟未來。

paragraphs.en: While policymakers debate the risks of falling behind, major businesses are already moving forward with their own AI plans. Lloyds Banking Group recently announced a significant hiring drive, looking for 300 new tech experts to help the bank develop and use "agentic AI." This term refers to autonomous artificial intelligence models that can plan and complete tasks with very little human help. According to The Guardian, the bank intends to use these tools to improve customer service, prevent fraud, and help employees search through large amounts of internal documents. Trystan Davies, the group head of data and AI science at Lloyds, noted that AI will change how organizations are structured and how people perform their jobs. While the bank is currently hiring, it has not ruled out the possibility that the broad use of AI could lead to job cuts in the future, a trend that has already been seen at other large financial institutions.
paragraphs.zh: 在決策者辯論落後風險的同時，大型企業已著手推動各自的人工智慧計畫。勞埃德銀行集團（Lloyds Banking Group）近期宣布大規模徵才，計畫招募三百名科技專家，協助銀行開發並運用「代理型人工智慧」。此術語指的是具備自主性的人工智慧模型，能在極少人工協助下規劃並完成任務。根據《衛報》報導，該銀行打算利用這些工具來提升客戶服務、防範詐欺，並協助員工搜尋龐大的內部文件。勞埃德銀行數據與人工智慧科學部門主管崔斯坦·戴維斯（Trystan Davies）指出，人工智慧將改變組織結構及人員執行工作的方式。儘管該銀行目前正在徵才，但並未排除人工智慧的廣泛應用可能在未來導致裁員的可能性，這股趨勢已在其他大型金融機構中出現。

paragraphs.en: However, the rapid adoption of these technologies brings significant risks. Research from KPMG suggests that while many bank executives feel confident about their ability to manage AI, few have actually tested their systems for potential failures. Rob Smith, the UK head of regulatory and risk advisory at KPMG, explained that the industry’s optimism might mean that firms either have strong safety plans in place, or that they do not yet fully understand the risks involved in their reliance on these complex tools. This uncertainty highlights the tension between the desire for efficiency and the need for reliable, safe systems.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，快速採用這些技術也帶來了重大風險。畢馬威會計師事務所（KPMG）的研究顯示，雖然許多銀行高層對管理人工智慧的能力感到自信，但鮮少有人真正針對潛在故障測試過其系統。畢馬威英國監管與風險諮詢主管羅伯·史密斯（Rob Smith）解釋，業界的樂觀態度可能意味著企業要麼已制定完善的安全計畫，要麼就是尚未完全理解依賴這些複雜工具所涉及的風險。這種不確定性凸顯了追求效率與需求可靠、安全系統之間的緊張關係。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the world of finance, the rise of AI is also affecting the arts. The literary magazine Granta recently announced it would stop publishing the winners of the Commonwealth short story prize. This decision follows widespread controversy after critics claimed that one of this year’s winning entries showed clear signs of being written by an AI. The magazine stated that it would no longer take part in partnerships where it does not have full control over the editorial process. While the author of the story, Jamir Nazir, strongly rejected these claims and explained that his process involved using speech-to-text technology due to health conditions, the incident has sparked a larger discussion about the future of human creativity. Sigrid Rausing, the publisher at Granta, noted that it may be difficult to ever truly know if a piece of work was created with the help of AI, which poses a difficult challenge for those who judge and reward human writing.
paragraphs.zh: 除了金融界，人工智慧的興起也影響了藝術領域。文學雜誌《格蘭塔》（Granta）近期宣布，將停止發布大英國協短篇小說獎的獲獎作品。此決定源於廣泛的爭議，此前有評論家聲稱，今年其中一篇獲獎作品有明顯由人工智慧撰寫的跡象。該雜誌表示，未來將不再參與無法完全掌控編輯流程的合作案。儘管該故事作者賈米爾·納齊爾（Jamir Nazir）強烈否認這些指控，並解釋因健康狀況而使用了語音轉文字技術，但此事件已引發關於人類創造力未來的廣泛討論。《格蘭塔》發行人西格麗德·勞辛（Sigrid Rausing）指出，或許很難真正確認某件作品是否在人工智慧協助下創作，這對評審與獎勵人類寫作的人士而言，是一項艱鉅的挑戰。

paragraphs.en: These events demonstrate that the impact of AI is being felt in every part of society. Whether it is a bank trying to improve its services or a magazine protecting its reputation, the challenge remains the same: how to use these powerful tools without losing the human element that is so important to our work. As the US and China continue to invest heavily in datacentres and robotics, European leaders are left to consider how they will compete in this new landscape. For now, the focus remains on finding a balance between the excitement of new technology and the need for careful, responsible growth. As these stories show, the path forward is not just about building faster computers, but also about understanding how these changes will affect our daily lives, our jobs, and our culture for years to come.
paragraphs.zh: 這些事件證明，人工智慧的影響已滲透到社會的每個角落。無論是試圖改善服務的銀行，還是維護聲譽的雜誌，挑戰始終如一：如何在不失去對工作至關重要的人性元素前提下，運用這些強大的工具。隨著美國與中國持續在資料中心與機器人技術上投入鉅資，歐洲領導人被迫思考如何在這個新局勢中競爭。目前，焦點仍放在如何在科技創新的興奮感與謹慎、負責任的成長需求之間取得平衡。正如這些故事所顯示，前進的道路不僅在於打造更快速的電腦，更在於理解這些變革將如何在未來多年內影響我們的日常生活、工作與文化。

keyTerms.definitionEn: An organized effort to achieve a specific goal.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （為達成目標的）有組織的行動、運動。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（駕駛），這裡作名詞，指大規模的招募活動。文中：Lloyds Banking Group recently announced a significant hiring drive, looking for 300 new tech experts to help the bank develop and use "agentic AI."

keyTerms.definitionEn: To decide that something is not possible or will not be considered.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由 rule 和 out 組成，意思與字面拆解不同，指排除某種可能性。文中：While the bank is currently hiring, it has not ruled out the possibility that the broad use of AI could lead to job cuts in the future, a trend that has already been seen at other large financial institutions.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be involved in an activity or event.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見的片語，但容易與「拆解」混淆，這裡指參與合作關係。文中：The magazine stated that it would no longer take part in partnerships where it does not have full control over the editorial process.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: A viral doomsday scenario aims to shake Europe out of its AI complacency

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Lloyds Banking Group to hire 300 tech experts to work on AI

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Granta stops publishing short story award winners over AI controversy

previousArticles.title: New Government Rules on AI Spark Debate Over Safety and Control
previousArticles.summaryEn: The U.S. government has issued new rules that forced the company Anthropic to shut down its most advanced AI models. These models, known as Fable 5 and Mythos 5, were found to be capable of performing complex cyber-attacks without human help. The government used export controls to restrict access, a move that experts say is unprecedented and unclear. While some argue that strict regulation is necessary to prevent dangerous outcomes, others worry that current methods are confusing and unsustainable. The situation highlights the growing tension between rapid technological progress and the need for safety.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason Lloyds Banking Group is recruiting 300 new tech experts?

options.A: To replace all human employees with autonomous AI models.
options.B: To develop and implement 'agentic AI' for internal and customer-facing tasks.
options.C: To compete directly with the US and China in building new datacentres.
options.D: To investigate potential failures in their existing regulatory systems.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，Lloyds Banking Group 聘請 300 名專家是為了開發並運用「agentic AI」，以提升客戶服務、防止詐騙及協助處理內部文件。選項 A 錯誤，因為銀行雖未排除未來裁員的可能性，但目前目標並非完全取代人類。選項 C 錯誤，文中提到 datacentres 是針對歐洲與美中競爭的背景，而非銀行的招聘動機。選項 D 錯誤，那是 KPMG 研究中提到的風險管理問題，與銀行的招聘目的不同。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the future of literary competitions regarding AI usage?

options.A: Magazines will likely ban all forms of technology, including speech-to-text tools.
options.B: It will become increasingly difficult for judges to verify the authenticity of human-authored works.
options.C: Most authors will eventually admit to using AI to improve their chances of winning.
options.D: The Commonwealth short story prize will be permanently cancelled due to the controversy.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第四段，Granta 的發行人 Sigrid Rausing 指出，未來可能很難辨別作品是否由 AI 輔助創作，這對評審機制構成了挑戰。選項 A 錯誤，文中僅提到該雜誌對編輯過程的控制權，未提及全面禁止所有科技。選項 C 錯誤，文中並未暗示作者會承認使用 AI，反而提到 Jamir Nazir 強烈否認。選項 D 錯誤，文章僅提到 Granta 退出該獎項的合作，並未說明獎項本身將被永久取消。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does the word 'optimism' imply in the context of bank executives' approach to AI?

options.A: An unverified belief that their systems are safe and under control.
options.B: A strategic plan to invest more money into AI infrastructure.
options.C: A feeling of excitement about the potential for job growth.
options.D: A clear understanding of the risks associated with new technology.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到 KPMG 的研究顯示，儘管主管們感到「optimism」（樂觀），但實際上很少人測試過系統，這暗示這種樂觀可能來自於對潛在風險的忽視或缺乏深入了解。選項 B 錯誤，樂觀情緒與投資金額無直接因果關係。選項 C 錯誤，文章並未提到主管對就業成長感到樂觀。選項 D 錯誤，文中正是因為缺乏測試，才暗示主管們對風險的理解並不充分，因此這與樂觀情緒的含義相反。

quiz.question: What is the central theme of the article regarding the rise of artificial intelligence?

options.A: The necessity for European countries to copy US and Chinese AI policies.
options.B: The inevitable decline of human creativity in the face of machine learning.
options.C: The challenge of balancing technological progress with safety and human values.
options.D: The financial benefits of adopting autonomous AI in the banking sector.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章從歐洲的競爭壓力、銀行的技術採用、風險管理的缺失到藝術界的審查挑戰，皆圍繞著一個核心議題：如何在追求 AI 帶來的效率與進步的同時，兼顧安全性、負責任的成長以及維護人類元素。選項 A 錯誤，文章僅提到歐洲需加強投資，未建議完全複製美中政策。選項 B 錯誤，文章僅討論挑戰，並未斷言人類創意必然衰退。選項 D 錯誤，金融業僅是文章討論的其中一個面向，並非全文主旨。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
